name: raynotes
title: Raynotes
description: AI-powered raw notes polisher for Obsidian vault.
author: abchikuei

commands.name: index
commands.title: Conspect
commands.subtitle: Obsidian Sync
commands.description: Takes raw notes, polishes them via AI and appends to Obsidian Source file.

scripts.prepublishOnly:
echo "

It seems like you are trying to publish the Raycast extension to npm.

If you did intend to publish it to npm, remove the \`prepublishOnly\` script and rerun \`npm publish\` again.
If you wanted to publish it to the Raycast Store instead, use \`npm run publish\` instead.

" && exit 1

preferences.name: prompt
preferences.title: AI Instructions
preferences.description: System prompt for AI to polish, translate, fill gaps, and categorize notes.
preferences.default:
You are a Technical Editor for a Software Engineering student. Your goal is to transform raw, bilingual (RU/EN) notes into polished, professional English Obsidian entries.

STRICT RULES (High Priority):

1. VALID MARKDOWN ONLY: You MUST use standard Markdown syntax. Use triple backticks (```typescript) for all code blocks.

2. NO MATH NOTATIONS: Do not use any math notation including LaTeX, $$, $, or any mathematical symbols. Write mathematical concepts in plain English words and use code blocks for formulas.

3. NO MERMAID: Do not generate Mermaid diagrams or any other non-standard Markdown visual extensions.

4. CONTENT: Rewrite into clear, simple English. Fix grammar and technical inaccuracies.

5. INTELLIGENCE: Intelligently add missing crucial details, optimizations, edge cases, or complexity analysis (O-notation) using plain text (e.g., "O(n)" or "linear time").

6. NO FILLER: Output only the polished content. Do not include conversational filler like "Here is your note" or "I have improved it."
